question: Give me the title and category name of films whose price per day is more than $1. Please include their special features.
evidence: category name refers to category.name; price per day is more than $1 refers to divide(rental_duration, rental_rate) > 1
SQL:
SELECT f.`title`,
       c.`name`,
       f.`special_features`
FROM `film` AS f
JOIN `film_category` AS fc ON f.`film_id` = fc.`film_id`
JOIN `category` AS c ON fc.`category_id` = c.`category_id`
WHERE f.`rental_rate` / f.`rental_duration` > 1;
original_question: Give me the title and category name of films whose price per day is more than $30. Please include their special features.
original_evidence: category name refers to category.name; price per day is more than $30 refers to multiply(rental_duration, rental_rate) > 30
original_SQL: SELECT T1.title, T3.name, T1.special_features FROM film AS T1 INNER JOIN film_category AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id INNER JOIN category AS T3 ON T2.category_id = T3.category_id WHERE T1.rental_duration * T1.rental_rate > 30

question: Among the routine inspections of Tiramisu Kitchen, how many of them have a score of over 70?
evidence: Tiramisu Kitchen is the name of the business; routine inspections refer to type LIKE 'Routine%'; score of over 70 refers to score > 70;
SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.business_id) FROM inspections AS T1 INNER JOIN businesses AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T2.name = 'Tiramisu Kitchen' AND T1.type LIKE 'Routine%' AND T1.score > 70
original_evidence: Tiramisu Kitchen is the name of the business; routine inspections refer to type = 'Routine - Unscheduled'; score of over 70 refers to score > 70;
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.business_id) FROM inspections AS T1 INNER JOIN businesses AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T2.name = 'Tiramisu Kitchen' AND T1.type = 'Routine - Unscheduled' AND T1.score > 70

question: How many professors teaches no more than two high-level or harder undergraduate courses?
evidence: professors refers to taughtBy.p_id; high-level or harder undergraduate courses  refers to courseLevel = 'Level_400' ; no more than two refers to count(taughtBy.course_id) < = 2
SQL:
SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM (
    SELECT p.`p_id`
    FROM `person` AS p
    LEFT JOIN `taughtBy` AS tb ON p.`p_id` = tb.`p_id`
    LEFT JOIN `course` AS c 
           ON tb.`course_id` = c.`course_id` 
          AND c.`courseLevel` = 'Level_400'
    WHERE p.`professor` = 1
    GROUP BY p.`p_id`
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.`course_id`) <= 2
);
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ( SELECT COUNT(T2.p_id) FROM course AS T1 INNER JOIN taughtBy AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T1.courseLevel = 'Level_400' GROUP BY T2.p_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.course_id) <= 2 )

question: Among the movie in which Dariusz Wolski works as the director of photography, what is the percentage of those movie whose vote average is over 5.0?
evidence: director of photography refers to job = 'Director of Photography'; vote average is over 8.0 refers to vote_average > 5; percentage = divide(sum(movie_id) when vote_average > 5, count(movie_id)) as percentage
SQL:
SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN vote_average > 5 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(vote_average) 
FROM (
  SELECT T1.vote_average vote_average
  FROM movie AS T1 
  INNER JOIN movie_crew AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id 
  INNER JOIN person AS T3 ON T2.person_id = T3.person_id 
  WHERE T3.person_name = 'Dariusz Wolski' AND T2.job = 'Director of Photography'
  GROUP BY T1.movie_id)
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.vote_average > 5 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.vote_average) FROM movie AS T1 INNER JOIN movie_crew AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id INNER JOIN person AS T3 ON T2.person_id = T3.person_id WHERE T3.person_name = 'Dariusz Wolski' AND T2.job = 'Director of Photography'

question: Among the episodes titled by "Gone Maggie Gone" in year 2009, what is the percentage of such episodes being nominated?
evidence: being nominated refers to result = 'Nominee'; percentage = divide(count(result = 'Nominee'), count(result)) * 100%
SQL: SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN A.result = 'Nominee' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_nominee FROM Award AS A JOIN Episode AS E ON A.episode_id = E.episode_id WHERE E.title = 'Gone Maggie Gone' AND A.year = 2009
original_question: In year 2009, what is the percentage of the episode titled by "Gone Maggie Gone" being nominated?
original_SQL: SELECT CAST((SUM(CASE WHEN T1.result = 'Nominee' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN T1.result = 'Winner' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.result) FROM Award AS T1 INNER JOIN Episode AS T2 ON T1.episode_id = T2.episode_id WHERE T2.title = 'Gone Maggie Gone' AND T1.year = 2009;

question: Please list the IDs of all the trains that run in the east direction and have less than 4 cars.
evidence: less than 4 cars refers to COUNT(cars) < 4
SQL:
SELECT t.id
FROM trains AS t
LEFT JOIN cars AS c ON t.id = c.train_id
WHERE t.direction = 'east'
GROUP BY t.id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) < 4;
original_evidence: less than 4 cars refers to carsNum < 4
original_SQL: SELECT T1.id FROM trains AS T1 INNER JOIN ( SELECT train_id, MAX(position) AS carsNum FROM cars GROUP BY train_id ) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T1.direction = 'east' AND T2.carsNum < 4

question: Among the listed cities, provide the area code of the city with the largest water area.

SQL: SELECT DISTINCT ac.`area_code` FROM zip_data zd JOIN ( SELECT `city`, SUM(`water_area`) AS total_water FROM zip_data GROUP BY `city` ) c  ON c.`city` = zd.`city` JOIN area_code ac ON ac.`zip_code` = zd.`zip_code` WHERE c.total_water = ( SELECT MAX(total_water) FROM ( SELECT `city`, SUM(`water_area`) AS total_water FROM zip_data GROUP BY `city` ) )

question: Name the teams played in a match which resulted in a tie in 2015.

SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.`Team_Name` FROM `Team` AS T1 JOIN `Match` AS M ON (T1.`Team_Id` = M.`Team_1` OR T1.`Team_Id` = M.`Team_2`) JOIN `Win_By` AS W ON M.`Win_Type` = W.`Win_Id` WHERE SUBSTR(M.`Match_Date`,1,4) = '2015' AND W.`Win_Type` = 'Tie';
original_SQL: SELECT T1.Team_Name FROM Team AS T1 INNER JOIN Match AS T2 ON T1.Team_Id = T2.Team_1 OR T1.Team_Id = T2.Team_2 INNER JOIN Win_By AS T3 ON T2.Win_Type = T3.Win_Id WHERE SUBSTR(T2.Match_Date, 1, 4) = '2015' AND T3.Win_Type = 'Tie' LIMIT 1

question: Who is the player that has the highest number of roles as a captain for Deccan Chargers?
evidence: name of the player refers to Player_Name; the highest number of roles refers to max(count(Role_Id)); as a captain refers to Role_Desc = 'Captain'; Deccan Chargers refers to Team_Name = 'Deccan Chargers'
SQL:
WITH player_num AS (
  SELECT T4.Player_Name name, COUNT(*) num
  FROM Team AS T1 
  INNER JOIN Player_Match AS T2 ON T1.Team_id = T2.Team_id 
  INNER JOIN Rolee AS T3 ON T2.Role_Id = T3.Role_Id 
  INNER JOIN Player AS T4 ON T2.Player_Id = T4.Player_Id 
  WHERE T1.Team_Name = 'Deccan Chargers' AND T1.Team_Id = 8 AND T3.Role_Desc = 'Captain' AND T3.Role_Id = 1 
  GROUP BY T4.Player_Id
) SELECT name FROM player_num WHERE num = (SELECT MAX(num) FROM player_num)
original_SQL: SELECT T4.Player_Name FROM Team AS T1 INNER JOIN Player_Match AS T2 ON T1.Team_id = T2.Team_id INNER JOIN Rolee AS T3 ON T2.Role_Id = T3.Role_Id INNER JOIN Player AS T4 ON T2.Player_Id = T4.Player_Id WHERE T1.Team_Name = 'Deccan Chargers' AND T1.Team_Id = 8 AND T3.Role_Desc = 'Captain' AND T3.Role_Id = 1 GROUP BY T4.Player_Id ORDER BY COUNT(T3.Role_Id) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Who were the actors that played in the movie "Batman" with the longest screentime?
evidence: movie "Batman" refers to Title = 'Batman'; longest screentime refers to max(screentime)
SQL: WITH t AS ( SELECT a.Name Name,  CAST(substr(c.`screentime`,1,instr(c.`screentime`,':')-1) AS INTEGER) hours, CAST(substr(c.screentime, -5, 2) AS INTEGER) minutes, CAST(substr(c.screentime, -2, 2) AS INTEGER) seconds FROM movie m join characters c on m.MovieID = c.MovieID join actor a on a.ActorID = c.ActorID where m.`Title` = 'Batman' AND c.`screentime` IS NOT NULL) SELECT Name FROM t WHERE hours*3600 + minutes * 60 + seconds = ( SELECT MAX(hours*3600 + minutes * 60 + seconds) FROM t );
original_question: Who was the actor that played in the movie "Batman" with the longest screentime?
original_SQL: SELECT T2.Name FROM characters AS T1 INNER JOIN actor AS T2 ON T1.ActorID = T2.ActorID INNER JOIN movie AS T3 ON T3.MovieID = T1.MovieID WHERE T3.Title = 'Batman' ORDER BY T1.screentime DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which part is ordered in a bigger amount in order no.1, "burnished seashell gainsboro navajo chocolate" or "salmon white grey tan navy"?
evidence: which part refers to part name; amount refers to sum(l_quantity); order no.1 refers to l_orderkey = 1; "burnished seashell gainsboro navajo chocolate" or "salmon white grey tan navy" refers to p_name IN ('salmon white grey tan navy', 'burnished seashell gainsboro navajo chocolate')
SQL: SELECT T.p_name FROM ( SELECT T2.p_name, SUM(T1.l_quantity) AS num FROM lineitem AS T1 INNER JOIN part AS T2 ON T1.l_partkey = T2.p_partkey WHERE T2.p_name IN ('salmon white grey tan navy', 'burnished seashell gainsboro navajo chocolate') AND T1.`l_orderkey` = 1 GROUP BY T1.l_partkey ) AS T ORDER BY T.num DESC LIMIT 1
original_evidence: amount refers to sum(l_quantity); order no.1 refers to l_orderkey = 1; "burnished seashell gainsboro navajo chocolate" or "salmon white grey tan navy" refers to p_name IN ('salmon white grey tan navy', 'burnished seashell gainsboro navajo chocolate')
original_SQL: SELECT T.p_name FROM ( SELECT T2.p_name, SUM(T1.l_quantity) AS num FROM lineitem AS T1 INNER JOIN part AS T2 ON T1.l_partkey = T2.p_partkey WHERE T2.p_name IN ('salmon white grey tan navy', 'burnished seashell gainsboro navajo chocolate') GROUP BY T1.l_partkey ) AS T ORDER BY T.num DESC LIMIT 1

question: How many hours in total did all of the Delta Air Lines aircraft were delayed due to a late aircraft in August of 2018?
evidence: hours in total = DIVIDE(SUM(LATE_AIRCRAFT_DELAY), 60); Delta Air Lines refers to Description = 'Delta Air Lines Inc.: DL'; delayed due to a late aircraft refers to LATE_AIRCRAFT_DELAY; in August of 2018 refers to FL_DATE like '2018/8/%';
SQL:
SELECT cast(SUM(`LATE_AIRCRAFT_DELAY`) as REAL)/60
  FROM `Airlines` AS a
  JOIN `Air Carriers` AS c ON c.`Code` = a.`OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID`
  WHERE c.`Description` = 'Delta Air Lines Inc.: DL'
    AND a.`FL_DATE` LIKE '2018/8/%'
original_question: How many hours in total did all of the Delta Air Lines aircraft were delayed due to a late aircraft in August of 2018? Identify the plane number of the aircraft with the highest delayed hours.
original_evidence: hours in total = DIVIDE(SUM(LATE_AIRCRAFT_DELAY), 60); Delta Air Lines refers to Description = 'Delta Air Lines Inc.: DL'; delayed due to a late aircraft refers to LATE_AIRCRAFT_DELAY; in August of 2018 refers to FL_DATE like '2018/8/%'; plane number refers to TAIL_NUM; highest delayed hours refers to MAX(DIVIDE(SUM(LATE_AIRCRAFT_DELAY),60));
original_SQL: SELECT T1.TAIL_NUM, SUM(CAST(T1.LATE_AIRCRAFT_DELAY AS REAL) / 60) AS delay FROM Airlines AS T1 INNER JOIN `Air Carriers` AS T2 ON T2.Code = T1.OP_CARRIER_AIRLINE_ID WHERE T1.FL_DATE LIKE '2018/8/%' AND T2.Description = 'Delta Air Lines Inc.: DL' ORDER BY delay DESC LIMIT 1

question: How many condiments were sold in 1997?
evidence: "Condiments" is the CategoryName; in 1997 refers to year(OrderDate) = 1997;
SQL: SELECT SUM(od.`Quantity`) AS total_condiments_sold FROM `OrderDetails` od JOIN `Orders` o ON od.`OrderID` = o.`OrderID` JOIN `Products` p ON od.`ProductID` = p.`ProductID` JOIN `Categories` c ON p.`CategoryID` = c.`CategoryID` WHERE c.`CategoryName` = 'Condiments' AND strftime('%Y', o.`OrderDate`) = '1997'
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.ProductID) FROM Categories AS T1 INNER JOIN Products AS T2 ON T1.CategoryID = T2.CategoryID INNER JOIN `Order Details` AS T3 ON T2.ProductID = T3.ProductID INNER JOIN Orders AS T4 ON T3.OrderID = T4.OrderID WHERE T1.CategoryName = 'Condiments' AND T1.CategoryID = 2 AND T4.OrderDate LIKE '1997%'

question: What is the total processed time of all solutions from the repository with the most forks?
evidence: total processed time = SUM(ProcessedTime where MAX(Forks)); repository with the most forks refers to MAX(Forks);
SQL: SELECT SUM(T2.ProcessedTime) FROM Repo AS T1 INNER JOIN Solution AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.RepoId WHERE T1.Forks = ( SELECT MAX(Forks) FROM Repo )

question: More crimes happened in which community area in January, 2018, Woodlawn or Lincoln Square?
evidence: in January 2018 refers to date like '1/%/2018%'; Woodlawn or Lincoln Square refers to community_area_name in ('Woodlawn', 'Lincoln Square'); number of crime refers to COUNT(report_no); the higher the report_no, the more crimes happened in the community;
SQL:
SELECT ca.community_area_name
FROM Community_Area AS ca
JOIN Crime AS c ON ca.community_area_no = c.community_area_no
WHERE ca.community_area_name IN ('Woodlawn', 'Lincoln Square')
  AND (
        c.`date` LIKE '1/%/2018%'   
      )
GROUP BY ca.community_area_name
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;
original_evidence: in January 2018 refers to date like '%1/2018%'; Woodlawn or Lincoln Square refers to community_area_name in ('Woodlawn', 'Lincoln Square'); number of crime refers to COUNT(report_no); the higher the report_no, the more crimes happened in the community;
original_SQL: SELECT T1.community_area_name FROM Community_Area AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T1.community_area_no = T2.community_area_no WHERE T1.community_area_name IN ('Woodlawn', 'Lincoln Square') AND T2.date LIKE '%1/2018%' GROUP BY T1.community_area_name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.community_area_name) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Among the active businesses located at Goodyear, AZ, list the category and attributes of businesses with a medium review count.

question: How many businesses are there in Phoenix city? Find the percentage of businesses in Phoenix city in the total city.
evidence: percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(city = 'Phoenix'), COUNT(business_id)), 100.0);
SQL:
SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS num_phoenix,
    CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) * 100.0 /
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `Business`) AS perc_phoenix
FROM `Business`
WHERE `city` = 'Phoenix';
original_evidence: percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(city = 'Phoenix' END), COUNT(category_id)), 1.0);
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN T3.city LIKE 'Phoenix' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "num" , CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T3.city LIKE 'Phoenix' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T3.city) FROM Business_Categories AS T1 INNER JOIN Categories AS T2 ON T1.category_id = T2.category_id INNER JOIN Business AS T3 ON T1.business_id = T3.business_id

question: What are the most widely used shipping companies in the United States of America?
evidence: most widely used refers to max(count(ShipVia)); shipping company refers to CompanyName; in the United States of America refers to ShipCountry = 'USA'
SQL: SELECT S.`ShipperName` FROM `Orders`  O JOIN `Customers` C ON O.`CustomerID` = C.`CustomerID` JOIN `Shippers`  S ON O.`ShipperID` = S.`ShipperID` WHERE C.`Country` = 'USA' GROUP BY S.`ShipperName` HAVING COUNT(*) = ( SELECT MAX(TotalOrders) FROM ( SELECT S2.`ShipperName`, COUNT(*) AS TotalOrders FROM `Orders`  O2 JOIN `Customers` C2 ON O2.`CustomerID` = C2.`CustomerID` JOIN `Shippers`  S2 ON O2.`ShipperID` = S2.`ShipperID` WHERE C2.`Country` = 'USA' GROUP BY S2.`ShipperName` ) AS ShipperOrderCounts );
original_question: What is the most widely used shipping company in the United States of America?
original_SQL: SELECT T2.CompanyName FROM Orders AS T1 INNER JOIN Shippers AS T2 ON T1.ShipVia = T2.ShipperID WHERE T1.ShipCountry = 'USA' GROUP BY T2.CompanyName ORDER BY COUNT(T2.CompanyName) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Calculate the total superstore's cost of the orders by Brad Thomas in 2016.
evidence: Brad Thomas is the "Customer Name"; in 2016 refers to strftime('%Y', "Order Date") = '2016'; total cost refers to SUM(SUTRACT(MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(Sales, SUTRACT(1, discount)), Quantity), Profit))
SQL:
WITH all_superstores AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM (
    SELECT * FROM `east_superstore`  WHERE strftime('%Y', `Order Date`) = '2016'
    UNION
    SELECT * FROM `west_superstore`  WHERE strftime('%Y', `Order Date`) = '2016'
    UNION
    SELECT * FROM `central_superstore` WHERE strftime('%Y', `Order Date`) = '2016'
    UNION
    SELECT * FROM `south_superstore`  WHERE strftime('%Y', `Order Date`) = '2016'
  ) t
) SELECT SUM((T1.Sales / (1 - T1.Discount)) * T1.Quantity - T1.Profit) AS cost FROM all_superstores AS T1 INNER JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.`Customer ID` = T2.`Customer ID` INNER JOIN product AS T3 ON T1.`Product ID` = T3.`Product ID` AND T1.Region = T3.Region WHERE T1.Region = 'East' AND T2.`Customer Name` = 'Brad Thomas'
original_question: Calculate the total cost of the orders by Brad Thomas in 2016.
original_SQL: SELECT SUM((T1.Sales / (1 - T1.Discount)) * T1.Quantity - T1.Profit) AS cost FROM east_superstore AS T1 INNER JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.`Customer ID` = T2.`Customer ID` INNER JOIN product AS T3 ON T1.`Product ID` = T3.`Product ID` AND T1.Region = T3.Region WHERE T1.Region = 'East' AND T2.`Customer Name` = 'Brad Thomas' AND strftime('%Y', T1.`Order Date`) = '2016'

question: Among the medals won by men’s-basketball players, what percentage were bronze?

question: Among the faculty employee professors, how many teaches high-level or harder undergraduate courses?
evidence: faculty employee professors refers to hasPosition = 'Faculty_eme' and professor = 1; high-level or harder undergraduate courses refers to courseLevel = 'Level_400'; professors unique identifying number refers to person.p_id
SQL:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.`p_id`) AS num_professors
FROM `person` AS T1
JOIN `taughtBy` AS T2  ON T1.`p_id` = T2.`p_id`
JOIN `course`   AS T3  ON T3.`course_id` = T2.`course_id`
WHERE T1.`hasPosition` = 'Faculty_eme'
  AND T1.`professor` = 1
  AND T3.`courseLevel` = 'Level_400';
original_question: Between the faculty employee professors, how many teaches high-level or harder undergraduate courses? Indicate each of the professors unique identifying number.
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM person AS T1 INNER JOIN taughtBy AS T2 ON T1.p_id = T2.p_id INNER JOIN course AS T3 ON T3.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T1.hasPosition = 'Faculty_eme' AND T1.professor = 1 AND T3.courseLevel = 'Level_400'

question: Which region has the most athletes?
evidence: region refers to region_name; the most athletes refer to MAX(COUNT(region_name));
SQL:
WITH region_num AS (
  SELECT T2.region_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.person_id) num
  FROM person_region AS T1 
  INNER JOIN noc_region AS T2 ON T1.region_id = T2.id 
  GROUP BY T2.region_name
) SELECT region_name FROM region_num WHERE num = (SELECT MAX(num) FROM region_num)
original_SQL: SELECT T2.region_name FROM person_region AS T1 INNER JOIN noc_region AS T2 ON T1.region_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.region_name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.person_id) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which year experienced the largest number of rainy days?
evidence: events LIKE 'Rain'; year refers to YEAR(date);
SQL:
SELECT SUBSTR(`date`, -4) AS yr
FROM weather
WHERE `events` LIKE 'Rain'
GROUP BY yr
ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT `date`) DESC
LIMIT 1;
original_question: Which year experienced the most rain?
original_evidence: events = 'Rain'; year refers to YEAR(date);
original_SQL: SELECT SUBSTR(CAST(date AS TEXT), -4) FROM weather GROUP BY SUBSTR(CAST(date AS TEXT), -4) ORDER BY SUM(CASE WHEN events LIKE '%Rain%' OR events LIKE '%rain%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Who has participated in the most Olympic Games in the database?
evidence: Who refers to full_name; participated in the most Olympic Games refers to MAX(COUNT(games_id));

WITH name_times AS (
  SELECT T1.full_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.games_id) num
  FROM person AS T1 
  INNER JOIN games_competitor AS T2

question: What is the tag and the artist of the most downloaded single?
evidence: the most downloaded single refers to MAX(totalSnatched where releaseType = 'single');
SQL:
SELECT t2.`tag`, t1.`artist`
FROM `torrents` AS t1
JOIN `tags` AS t2 ON t1.`id` = t2.`id`
WHERE t1.`releaseType` = 'single'
  AND t1.`totalSnatched` = (
        SELECT MAX(`totalSnatched`)
        FROM `torrents`
        WHERE `releaseType` = 'single'
      );
original_SQL: SELECT T2.tag, T1.artist FROM torrents AS T1 INNER JOIN tags AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.id WHERE T1.releaseType = 'single' ORDER BY T1.totalSnatched DESC LIMIT 1

question: Please list the business IDs of the Yelp_Business that have a business time of longer than 12 hours on Sundays.
evidence: business time of longer than 12 hours refers to subtract(closing_time, opening_time) > 12; on Sundays refers to day_of_week = 'Sunday'
SQL: WITH time_conversions AS (SELECT bh.business_id, CASE WHEN bh.opening_time LIKE '%AM' THEN CASE WHEN REPLACE(bh.opening_time, 'AM', '') = '12' THEN 0 ELSE CAST(REPLACE(bh.opening_time, 'AM', '') AS INT) END ELSE CASE WHEN REPLACE(bh.opening_time, 'PM', '') = '12' THEN 12 ELSE CAST(REPLACE(bh.opening_time, 'PM', '') AS INT) + 12 END END AS open_hr, CASE WHEN bh.closing_time LIKE '%AM' THEN CASE WHEN REPLACE(bh.closing_time, 'AM', '') = '12' THEN 0 ELSE CAST(REPLACE(bh.closing_time, 'AM', '') AS INT) END ELSE CASE WHEN REPLACE(bh.closing_time, 'PM', '') = '12' THEN 12 ELSE CAST(REPLACE(bh.closing_time, 'PM', '') AS INT) + 12 END END AS close_hr FROM Business_Hours bh JOIN Days d ON bh.day_id = d.day_id WHERE d.day_of_week = 'Sunday') SELECT business_id FROM time_conversions WHERE CASE WHEN close_hr >= open_hr THEN close_hr - open_hr ELSE 24 - (open_hr - close_hr) END > 12;
original_SQL: SELECT T1.business_id FROM Business_Hours AS T1 INNER JOIN Days AS T2 ON T1.day_id = T2.day_id INNER JOIN Business AS T3 ON T1.business_id = T3.business_id WHERE T1.closing_time + 12 - T1.opening_time > 12 AND T2.day_of_week LIKE 'Sunday' GROUP BY T1.business_id

question: Among the transactions, what percentage is done by using a visa card?

question: For all trips which took less 5 minutes, state the station name where the bike were borrowed and returned. Indicate mean temperature of the day.
evidence: less than 5 minutes refers to duration<300 in seconds; the station where the bike was borrowed refers to start_station_name; the station where the bike was returned refers to end_station_name; mean temperature refers to mean_temperature_f;
SQL:
SELECT
    t.start_station_name,
    t.end_station_name,
    w.mean_temperature_f
FROM trip   AS t
JOIN weather AS w
  ON w.zip_code = t.zip_code
 AND w.date = substr(t.start_date, 1, instr(t.start_date, ' ') - 1)
WHERE t.duration < 300;
original_evidence: less than 5 minutes refers to duration<300 in seconds; the station where the bike was borrowed refers to start_station_name; the station where the bike was returned refers to end_station_name; mean temperature refers to min_temperature_f;
original_SQL: SELECT T1.start_station_name, T1.end_station_name, T2.mean_temperature_f FROM trip AS T1 INNER JOIN weather AS T2 ON T2.zip_code = T1.zip_code WHERE T1.duration < 300

question: Which year was the third movie directed by Quentin Tarantino released?
evidence: third movie refers to third movie that has oldest movie_release_year;

SELECT movie_release_year 
FROM movies 
WHERE director_name = 'Quentin Tarantino' 
ORDER BY movie_release_year ASC LIMIT 2, 1
original_question: Which year was the third movie directed by Quentin Tarantino released? Indicate the user ids of the user who gave it a rating score of 4.

question: Who were the coaches for the teams which had the most bench minors penalty in 2003？
evidence: Coach of the team refers to firstName+lastName; 2003 refers to the year
SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T3.firstName, T3.lastName FROM Teams AS T1 INNER JOIN Coaches AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year INNER JOIN Master AS T3 ON T2.coachID = T3.coachID WHERE T1.year = '2003' GROUP BY T3.firstName, T3.lastName HAVING SUM(T1.BenchMinor) = (SELECT SUM(T1.BenchMinor) FROM Teams AS T1 INNER JOIN Coaches AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year INNER JOIN Master AS T3 ON T2.coachID = T3.coachID WHERE T1.year = '2003' GROUP BY T3.firstName, T3.lastName ORDER BY SUM(T1.BenchMinor) DESC LIMIT 1);
original_question: Who was the coach for the team which had the most bench minors penalty in 2003？
original_evidence: Coach of the team refers to firstName+lastName; 2003 refers to the year
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T3.firstName, T3.lastName FROM Teams AS T1 INNER JOIN Coaches AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year INNER JOIN Master AS T3 ON T2.coachID = T3.coachID WHERE T1.year = '2003' GROUP BY T3.firstName, T3.lastName ORDER BY SUM(T1.BenchMinor) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Please list the teams that have played in 1922's Stanley Cup finals.
evidence: teams refer to name; year = 1922; Stanley Cup finals refer to round='SCF'
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT T.name
FROM (
        SELECT tmIDWinner AS tmID
        FROM SeriesPost
        WHERE year = 1922 AND round = 'SCF'
        UNION ALL
        SELECT tmIDLoser AS tmID
        FROM SeriesPost
        WHERE year = 1922 AND round = 'SCF'
     ) AS F
JOIN Teams AS T
  ON T.year = 1922 AND T.tmID = F.tmID;
original_evidence: teams refer to tmID; year = 1922;
original_SQL: SELECT T2.name FROM TeamsSC AS T1 INNER JOIN Teams AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year WHERE T1.year = '1922'

question: In the least populated community, what is the most common location of all the reported crime incidents?
evidence: least populated refers to Min(Population); community refers to community_area_no; most common location refers to Max(Count(location_description))
SQL:
WITH least_pop AS (
    SELECT community_area_no
    FROM Community_Area
    ORDER BY CAST(REPLACE(population, ',', '') AS INTEGER) ASC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT location_description
FROM Crime
WHERE community_area_no = (SELECT community_area_no FROM least_pop)
GROUP BY location_description
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;
original_SQL: SELECT T2.location_description FROM Community_Area AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T1.community_area_no = T2.community_area_no WHERE T1.population = ( SELECT MIN(population) FROM Community_Area ) AND T2.location_description IS NOT NULL GROUP BY T2.location_description

question: List the names and calculate their profit for products with the highest rating in review.
evidence: Profit = SUBTRACT(ListPrice, StandardCost); the highest rating in review refers to MAX(Rating)
SQL: SELECT p.`Name`, p.`ListPrice` - p.`StandardCost` AS profit FROM   `Product` p JOIN   `ProductReview` pr ON p.`ProductID` = pr.`ProductID` WHERE  pr.`Rating` = (SELECT MAX(`Rating`) FROM `ProductReview`)
original_question: List the name and calculate its profit for product with the highest rating in review.
original_evidence: Profit = SUBTRACT(ListPrice, StandardCost); the highest rating in review refers to Rating = 5
original_SQL: SELECT T1.Name, T1.ListPrice - T1.StandardCost FROM Product AS T1 INNER JOIN ProductReview AS T2 ON T1.ProductID = T2.ProductID ORDER BY T2.Rating DESC LIMIT 1

question: Provide the directions for all the trains that have 2 or less cars.
evidence: 2 or less cars refers to COUNT(cars) < = 2
SQL:
SELECT t.direction
FROM trains AS t
LEFT JOIN cars AS c ON t.id = c.train_id
GROUP BY t.id
HAVING COUNT(c.id) <= 2;
original_evidence: 2 or less cars refers to trailPosi < = 2
original_SQL: SELECT T1.direction FROM trains AS T1 INNER JOIN ( SELECT train_id, MAX(position) AS trailPosi FROM cars GROUP BY train_id ) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id WHERE T2.trailPosi <= 2

question: Provide the name of the airport which landed the most number of flights on 2018/8/15.

SQL:

FROM `Airports`  A
JOIN `Airlines`  L ON A.`Code` = L.`DEST`

ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which cities in the Midwest region have the least number of clients?
evidence: least number of clients refers to min(count(client_id))
SQL: SELECT T2.city FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN state AS T3 ON T2.state_abbrev = T3.StateCode WHERE T3.Region = 'Midwest' GROUP BY T2.city HAVING COUNT(T1.client_id) = ( SELECT MIN(client_count) FROM ( SELECT COUNT(T1.client_id) AS client_count FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN state AS T3 ON T2.state_abbrev = T3.StateCode WHERE T3.Region = 'Midwest' GROUP BY T2.city ) AS ClientCounts );
original_question: Which city in the Midwest region has the least number of clients?
original_SQL: SELECT T2.city FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN state AS T3 ON T2.state_abbrev = T3.StateCode WHERE T3.Region = 'Midwest' GROUP BY T2.city ORDER BY COUNT(T2.city) LIMIT 1

question: In the film with an inventory ID between 20 to 60, how many of the films have a G rating?
evidence: G rating refers to rating = 'G'
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.film_id) FROM film AS T1 INNER JOIN inventory AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id WHERE T2.inventory_id BETWEEN 20 AND 60 AND T1.rating = 'G'
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.film_id) FROM film AS T1 INNER JOIN inventory AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id WHERE T2.inventory_id BETWEEN 20 AND 60 AND T1.rating = 'G'

question: What is the average replacement cost for the movies with a rental rate of 4.99?
evidence: a rental rate of 4.99 refers to rental_rate = 4.99; average replacement cost = avg(replacement_cost)
SQL: SELECT AVG(replacement_cost) FROM film WHERE rental_rate = 4.99

question: Indicate the coordinates of all the deserts whose area is in more than one country.
evidence: coordinates consists of Latitude, Longitude
SQL:
SELECT d.Latitude, d.Longitude
FROM desert d JOIN geo_desert g ON d.Name = g.Desert
GROUP BY d.Name, d.Latitude, d.Longitude
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT g.Country) > 1;
original_SQL: SELECT T1.Latitude, T1.Longitude FROM desert AS T1 INNER JOIN geo_desert AS T2 ON T1.Name = T2.Desert GROUP BY T1.Name, T1.Latitude, T1.Longitude HAVING COUNT(T1.Name) > 1

question: What are the content ratings for the apps that have "gr8" in their comments?

SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.`Content Rating` FROM playstore AS T1 INNER JOIN user_reviews AS T2 ON T1.App = T2.App WHERE T2.Translated_Review LIKE '%gr8%'

question: For the solution of the most 'sw' methods, what is its path?
evidence: solution refers to SolutionId; sw refers to Lang = 'sw'; solution with the most 'sw' methods refers to MAX(COUNT(Lang = 'sw'));
SQL: WITH sw_counts AS (SELECT `SolutionId`, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM `Method` WHERE `Lang` = 'sw' GROUP BY `SolutionId`), max_cnt AS (SELECT MAX(cnt) AS max_cnt FROM sw_counts) SELECT `S`.`Path` FROM `Solution` AS `S` JOIN sw_counts AS `C` ON `C`.`SolutionId` = `S`.`Id` JOIN max_cnt AS `M` ON `C`.`cnt` = `M`.`max_cnt`;
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Path FROM Solution AS T1 INNER JOIN Method AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.SolutionId WHERE T2.Lang = 'sw'

question: List down app that does not have negative sentiment and calculate the average rating for each app?
evidence: doest not have negative sentiment refers to Sentiment! = 'Negative'; average = AVG(Sentiment_Polarity);
SQL:
SELECT `App`,
       AVG(CAST(`Sentiment_Polarity` AS REAL)) AS avg_polarity
FROM `user_reviews`
GROUP BY `App`
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN `Sentiment` = 'Negative' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0;
original_question: List down app that does not have negative sentiment and give their average rating?
original_SQL: SELECT T1.App, AVG(T2.Sentiment_Polarity) FROM playstore AS T1 INNER JOIN user_reviews AS T2 ON T1.App = T2.App WHERE T2.Sentiment != 'Negative' GROUP BY T1.App

question: How many domestic crime cases has Commander Ronald A. Pontecore Jr. been responsible for investigating?

question: Calculate the percentage of Asian students among students of all races who graduated from institution in Alabama in year 2013 within 100 percent of normal / expected time.
evidence: Asian refers to race = 'A'; Alabama refers to state = 'Alabama'; graduated within 100 percent of normal/expected time refers to grad_100; percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(grad_100 when race = 'A'), SUM(grad_100 when race='X')), 100);
SQL:
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN g.race = 'A' THEN CAST(g.grad_100 AS INTEGER) ELSE 0 END)
              / SUM(CASE WHEN g.race = 'X' THEN CAST(g.grad_100 AS INTEGER) ELSE 0 END) AS pct_of_asian_vs_other
        FROM `institution_details` d
        JOIN `institution_grads` g ON d.`unitid` = g.`unitid`
        WHERE d.`state` = 'Alabama'
          AND g.`year` = 2013
          AND g.`grad_100` IS NOT NULL;
original_question: Calculate the percentage of Asian students among students of other races who graduated from institution in Alabama in year 2013 within 100 percent of normal / expected time.
original_evidence: Asian refers to race = 'A'; Alabama refers to state = 'Alabama'; graduated within 100 percent of normal/expected time refers to grad_100; percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(race = 'A'), SUM(grad_cohort)), 100);
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.race = 'A' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(T2.grad_cohort) FROM institution_details AS T1 INNER JOIN institution_grads AS T2 ON T1.unitid = T2.unitid WHERE T2.year = 2013 AND T1.state = 'Alabama'

question: Calculate the average game sales for the PS2 platform.
evidence: average = AVG(MULTIPLY(num_sales), 100000); PS2 refers to platform_name = 'PS2';
SQL: SELECT AVG(T3.num_sales * 100000) FROM platform AS T1 INNER JOIN game_platform AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.platform_id INNER JOIN region_sales AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.game_platform_id WHERE T1.platform_name = 'PS2'
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(T3.num_sales * 100000) / COUNT(T1.id) FROM platform AS T1 INNER JOIN game_platform AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.platform_id INNER JOIN region_sales AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.game_platform_id WHERE T1.platform_name = 'PS2'

question: What is the level of the course with the most number of teachers?
evidence: level of the course refers to courseLevel; course with most number of teachers refers to course_id = max(count(taughtBy.p_id))
SQL:
SELECT T1.courseLevel FROM course AS T1 INNER JOIN taughtBy AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T2.course_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.p_id) = ( 
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T4.p_id) FROM course AS T3 INNER JOIN taughtBy AS T4 ON T3.course_id = T4.course_id GROUP BY T4.course_id ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T4.p_id) DESC LIMIT 1)
original_SQL: SELECT T1.courseLevel FROM course AS T1 INNER JOIN taughtBy AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T2.course_id ORDER BY COUNT(T2.p_id) DESC LIMIT 1

FROM `Ward`

question: After Azha Restaurant Inc. passed the inspection on 2010/1/21, when was the follow-up inspection done?

SQL: SELECT i2.`inspection_date` FROM `inspection`  AS i1 JOIN `inspection`  AS i2 ON i2.`followup_to` = i1.`inspection_id` JOIN `establishment` AS e  ON e.`license_no` = i1.`license_no` WHERE e.`dba_name` = 'Azha Restaurant Inc.' AND i1.`inspection_date` = '2010-01-21' AND i1.`results` = 'Pass'

question: What is the total value of shipped vintage car orders from 2003-2004?
evidence: total value = SUM(MULTIPLY(quantityOrdered, priceEach)); shipped orders refers to status = 'Shipped'; vintage car is a product line; year(orderDate) between 2003 and 2004;
SQL: SELECT SUM(T2.priceEach * T2.quantityOrdered) FROM products AS T1 INNER JOIN orderdetails AS T2 ON T1.productCode = T2.productCode INNER JOIN orders AS T3 ON T2.orderNumber = T3.orderNumber WHERE T3.status = 'Shipped' AND T3.orderDate BETWEEN '2003-01-01' AND '2004-12-31' AND T1.productLine = 'Vintage Cars'
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(T2.priceEach * T2.quantityOrdered) FROM products AS T1 INNER JOIN orderdetails AS T2 ON T1.productCode = T2.productCode INNER JOIN orders AS T3 ON T2.orderNumber = T3.orderNumber WHERE T3.status = 'Shipped' AND T3.orderDate BETWEEN '2003-01-01' AND '2004-12-31'

question: What are the names of the most expensive cars?
evidence: name of the car refers to car_name; the most expensive refers to max(price)
SQL: SELECT T1.car_name FROM data AS T1 INNER JOIN price AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.price = (SELECT MAX(T2.price) FROM data AS T1 INNER JOIN price AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID);
original_question: What is the name of the most expensive car?
original_SQL: SELECT T1.car_name FROM data AS T1 INNER JOIN price AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID ORDER BY T2.price DESC LIMIT 1

question: Among all publications containing keywords 'Turbulent Flows', what percentage of them was published in the journal named 'Physics of Fluids'?
evidence: 'Physics of Fluids' is the FullName of journal; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(Keyword contains 'Turbulent Flows' AND FullName = 'Physics of Fluids'), SUM(Keyword contains 'Turbulent Flows'))*100 as percentage
SQL:
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN j.`FullName` = 'Physics of Fluids' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS percentage
FROM `Paper` p
JOIN `Journal` j ON p.`JournalId` = j.`Id`
WHERE p.`Keyword` LIKE '%Turbulent Flows%'
original_question: Among all publications containing keywords 'Turbulent Fluids', what percentage of them was published in the journal named 'Physics of Fluids'?
original_evidence: 'Physics of Fluids' is the FullName of journal; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(Keyword = 'Turbulent Fluids'), SUM(FullName = 'Physics of Fluids')) as percentage
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.Keyword = 'Turbulent Fluids' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T2.FullName = 'Physics of Fluids' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM Paper AS T1 INNER JOIN Journal AS T2 ON T1.JournalId = T2.Id

question: What are the names of players who had been man of the match more than 3 times in season year 2008?
evidence: man of the match more than 5 times refers to COUNT(Man_of_the_Match) > 5; in season year 2008 refers to Season_Year = 2008; name of player refers to Player_Name;
SQL:
SELECT p.`Player_Name`
FROM `Match`  m
JOIN `Season` s ON m.`Season_Id` = s.`Season_Id`
JOIN `Player` p ON m.`Man_of_the_Match` = p.`Player_Id`
WHERE s.`Season_Year` = 2008
GROUP BY p.`Player_Id`
HAVING COUNT(*) > 3;
original_question: What are the names of players who had been man of the match more than 5 times in season year 2008?
original_SQL: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(T2.Man_of_the_Match) > 5 THEN T1.Player_Name ELSE 0 END FROM Player AS T1 INNER JOIN Match AS T2 ON T1.Player_Id = T2.Man_of_the_Match INNER JOIN Player_Match AS T3 ON T3.Player_Id = T1.Player_Id INNER JOIN Season AS T4 ON T2.Season_Id = T4.Season_Id WHERE T4.Season_Year = 2008

question: What is first name for player 'aebisda01'. Calculate the average time in minutes for the all his games played as goaltender.
evidence: played as goaltender refers to pos = 'G'; time in minutes refers to Min; all his games played refers to GP; average time in minutes refers to DIVIDE(SUM(Min)/SUM(GP))
SQL:
SELECT
     M.`firstName`,
    SUM(CAST(G.`Min` AS REAL)) / SUM(CAST(G.`GP` AS REAL)) AS avg_minutes
FROM `Master` AS M
JOIN `Goalies` AS G ON M.`playerID` = G.`playerID`
WHERE M.`playerID` = 'aebisda01'
GROUP BY M.`playerID`,  M.`firstName`;
original_question: What is given name for player 'aebisda01'. Calculate the average time in minutes for the all his games played as goaltender.
original_SQL: SELECT T1.nameGiven, CAST(SUM(T2.Min) AS REAL) / SUM(T2.GP) FROM Master AS T1 INNER JOIN Goalies AS T2 ON T1.playerID = T2.playerID WHERE T1.playerID = 'aebisda01' GROUP BY T1.nameGiven

question: What percentage of genes located in the cytoskeleton are of unknown class? And of these, how many are not conditional phenotypes?
evidence: Percentage = count(genes located in the cytoskeleton unknown class) / count(genes located in the cytoskeleton) * 100%; unknown class refers to Class IN ('Unknown','?')
SQL:
WITH cytoskel AS (
    SELECT `GeneID`,
           MAX(CASE WHEN `Class` IN ('Unknown','?') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS is_unknown,
           MAX(CASE WHEN `Phenotype`='Conditional phenotypes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS is_conditional
    FROM `Genes`
    WHERE `Localization`='cytoskeleton'
    GROUP BY `GeneID`
)
SELECT
    100.0 * SUM(is_unknown) / COUNT(*) AS pct_unknown_class,
    SUM(CASE WHEN is_unknown=1 AND is_conditional=0 THEN 1 END) AS not_conditional_unknown
FROM cytoskel;
original_evidence: Percentage = count(genes located in the cytoskeleton unknown class) / count(genes located in the cytoskeleton) * 100%
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(Localization = 'cytoskeleton' AND Phenotype = 'Conditional phenotypes') , CAST(SUM(Localization = 'cytoskeleton') AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(GeneID) FROM Genes;

question: What is the proportion of horror movies among all movies genre?

original_evidence: horror movies refers to genre_name = 'horror'; average number = divide(sum(movie_id) when genre_name = 'horror', count(movie_id))

question: What is the medium through which most complaints are registered in Florida?
evidence: medium refers to "Submitted via"; most complaints refers to MAX(Complaint ID); Florida refers to state = 'florida';
SQL:
SELECT E.`Submitted via`
FROM `events` AS E
JOIN `client`  AS C ON E.`Client_ID` = C.`client_id`
WHERE C.`state` = 'FL'
GROUP BY E.`Submitted via`
HAVING COUNT(*) = (
  SELECT COUNT(*)
  FROM `events` AS E
  JOIN `client`  AS C ON E.`Client_ID` = C.`client_id`
  WHERE C.`state` = 'FL'
  GROUP BY E.`Submitted via`
  ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1
)
original_SQL: SELECT T3.`Submitted via` FROM callcenterlogs AS T1 INNER JOIN client AS T2 ON T1.`rand client` = T2.client_id INNER JOIN events AS T3 ON T1.`Complaint ID` = T3.`Complaint ID` WHERE T2.state = 'FL' GROUP BY T1.`Complaint ID` ORDER BY COUNT(T1.`Complaint ID`) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Among the employees that receive a salary between $75000 to $85000, what is the difference between the number of employees which undergone an inspection that fined 100 and 500?
evidence: salary between $75000 and $85000 refers to 75000 < = salary < = 85000; difference = subtract(count(inspection_id where fine = 100), count(inspection_id where fine = 500)) where 75000 < = salary < = 85000
SQL:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN fine_100 = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) -
  SUM(CASE WHEN fine_500 = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS diff
FROM (
    SELECT e.`employee_id`,
           MAX(CASE WHEN v.`fine` = 100 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fine_100,
           MAX(CASE WHEN v.`fine` = 500 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fine_500
    FROM `employee` e
    JOIN `inspection` i ON e.`employee_id` = i.`employee_id`
    JOIN `violation` v ON i.`inspection_id` = v.`inspection_id`
    WHERE e.`salary` BETWEEN 75000 AND 85000
    GROUP BY e.`employee_id`
) sub;
original_evidence: salary between $75000 and $85000 refers to 75000 < = salary < = 80000; difference = subtract(count(inspection_id where fine = 100), count(inspection_id where fine = 500)) where 75000 < = salary < = 80000
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN T3.fine = 100 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN T3.fine = 500 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM employee AS T1 INNER JOIN inspection AS T2 ON T1.employee_id = T2.employee_id INNER JOIN violation AS T3 ON T2.inspection_id = T3.inspection_id WHERE T1.salary BETWEEN 75000 AND 80000

question: Based on the latest pay rate for each employee, calculate the average hourly pay rate for each department. Provide the department ID, department name, and the average hourly pay rate.
evidence: latest payrate refers to max(RateChangeDate); average hourly payrate = divide(sum(Rate), count(BusinessEntityID)) for each DepartmentID
SQL:
SELECT d.DepartmentID,
       d.Name,
       AVG(eph.Rate) AS AvgLatestRate
FROM EmployeeDepartmentHistory   edh
JOIN Department              d   ON d.DepartmentID = edh.DepartmentID
JOIN EmployeePayHistory      eph ON eph.BusinessEntityID = edh.BusinessEntityID
WHERE edh.EndDate IS NULL                          -- current dept (optional)
  AND eph.RateChangeDate = (
        SELECT MAX(eph2.RateChangeDate)
        FROM EmployeePayHistory eph2
        WHERE eph2.BusinessEntityID = eph.BusinessEntityID)
GROUP BY d.DepartmentID, d.Name;
original_question: Based on the lastet payrate of each employee, calculate the average hourly payrate for each department.
original_SQL: SELECT AVG(T1.Rate) FROM EmployeePayHistory AS T1 INNER JOIN EmployeeDepartmentHistory AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID INNER JOIN Department AS T3 ON T2.DepartmentID = T3.DepartmentID WHERE T1.RateChangeDate = ( SELECT MAX(T1.RateChangeDate) FROM EmployeePayHistory AS T1 INNER JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.DepartmentID )

question: Calculate the percentage of the surface area of all countries that uses Chinese as one of their languages.
evidence: percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(SurfaceArea WHERE Language = 'Chinese'), SUM(SurfaceArea)), 100); Chinese as one of the languages refers to Language = 'Chinese';
SQL:
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(`SurfaceArea`)
       / (SELECT SUM(`SurfaceArea`) FROM `Country`)
FROM `Country`
WHERE `Code` IN (
      SELECT `CountryCode`
      FROM `CountryLanguage`
      WHERE `Language` = 'Chinese');
original_evidence: percentage = DIVIDE(MULTIPLY(SUM(SurfaceArea WHERE Language = 'Chinese'), SUM(SurfaceArea)), 1.0); Chinese as one of the languages refers to Language = 'Chinese';
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T2.Language = 'Chinese', T1.SurfaceArea, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(T1.SurfaceArea) FROM Country AS T1 INNER JOIN CountryLanguage AS T2 ON T1.Code = T2.CountryCode

question: From 1968 to 1970, what are indicator names whose license type is open and values are less than 100?
evidence: From 1968 to 1970 refers to Year between '1968' and '1970'; values are less than 100 imply Value<100;
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT i.`IndicatorName`
FROM `Indicators` i
JOIN `Series`  s ON i.`IndicatorCode` = s.`SeriesCode`
WHERE i.`Year` BETWEEN 1968 AND 1970
  AND s.`LicenseType` = 'Open'
  AND i.`Value` < 100;
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.IndicatorName FROM Indicators AS T1 INNER JOIN Series AS T2 ON T1.IndicatorName = T2.IndicatorName WHERE T1.Year >= 1968 AND T1.Year < 1971 AND T2.LicenseType = 'Open' AND T1.Value < 100

question: List the case numbers of crimes that occurred in wards with populations below 52,000 whose beat is greater than 90% of the average beat of all crimes in those wards.
evidence: population below 52000 refers to population < 52000; beat greater than the 90% of the average beat refers to beat > multiply(avg(beat), 0.9) where population < 52000
SQL:
WITH avg_beat AS (
    SELECT AVG(T1.beat) as avg_be FROM Crime AS T1 INNER JOIN Ward AS T2 ON T1.ward_no = T2.ward_no WHERE CAST(T2.Population  AS INTEGER) < 52000
)
SELECT DISTINCT c.`case_number`
FROM `Crime` AS c
JOIN `Ward` AS w ON c.`ward_no` = w.`ward_no`
JOIN avg_beat AS a
WHERE CAST(w.`Population` AS INTEGER) < 52000
  AND c.`beat` > 0.9 * a.`avg_be`;
original_question: List the case number of the crimes in wards with population below 52000 that have beat greater than the 90% of the average beat of all crimes.
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.report_no) FROM Crime AS T1 INNER JOIN Ward AS T2 ON T1.ward_no = T2.ward_no WHERE T2.Population < 52000 AND T1.beat > ( SELECT AVG(T1.beat) * 0.9 FROM Crime AS T1 INNER JOIN Ward AS T2 ON T1.ward_no = T2.ward_no WHERE T2.Population < 52000 )

question: How many employees who are living in Australia and have at least one customer whose credit limit is under 200000?

FROM (
  SELECT DISTINCT e.`employeeNumber`
  FROM `employees` e
  JOIN `offices`  o ON e.`officeCode` = o.`officeCode`
  JOIN `customers` c ON c.`salesRepEmployeeNumber` = e.`employeeNumber`
  WHERE o.`country` = 'Australia'
    AND c.`creditLimit` < 200000
) sub;

question: In Albany Park, how many arrests were made in an apartment due to criminal sexual abuse?
evidence: Albany Park refers to district_name = 'Albany Park'; in an apartment refers to location_description = 'APARTMENT'; criminal sexual abuse refers to title = 'Criminal Sexual Abuse'
SQL: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN T3.title = 'Criminal Sexual Abuse' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM District AS T1 INNER JOIN Crime AS T2 ON T1.district_no = T2.district_no INNER JOIN FBI_Code AS T3 ON T2.fbi_code_no = T3.fbi_code_no WHERE T1.district_name = 'Albany Park' AND T2.arrest = 'TRUE' AND T2.location_description = 'APARTMENT'

question: Find the average ride duration during the rain of more than 0.8 inches.
evidence: rain of more than 0.8 inches refers to events = rain where precipitation_inches>0.8; average ride duration = DIVIDE(SUM(duration), COUNT(duration));
SQL:
SELECT AVG(t.`duration`)  
FROM `trip`  AS t
JOIN  `weather` AS w
  ON  w.`date` = SUBSTR(t.`start_date`,1,INSTR(t.`start_date`,' ')-1)   -- same day
  AND w.`zip_code` = t.`zip_code`                                        -- same location
WHERE w.`events` LIKE 'Rain'
  AND (CASE WHEN w.`precipitation_inches` IN ('T','0')
            THEN 0
            ELSE CAST(w.`precipitation_inches` AS REAL) END) > 0.8;
original_SQL: SELECT AVG(T1.duration) FROM trip AS T1 INNER JOIN weather AS T2 ON T2.zip_code = T1.zip_code WHERE (T2.events = 'Rain' AND T2.precipitation_inches > 0.8) OR (T2.events = 'rain' AND T2.precipitation_inches > 0.8)

question: Please give more detailed note information about the first four books that sell the best.
evidence: qty is abbreviation for quantity; sells the best mean with the most sales quantity; MAX(SUM(qty))
SQL:
SELECT t.notes
FROM `titles` t
JOIN (
    SELECT `title_id`, SUM(`qty`) AS total_qty
    FROM `sales`
    GROUP BY `title_id`
    ORDER BY total_qty DESC
    LIMIT 4
) s ON t.`title_id` = s.`title_id`;
original_question: Please give more detailed information about the first three books that sell the best.
original_evidence: qty is abbreviation for quantity; sells the best mean with the most sales quantity; MAX(qty)
original_SQL: SELECT T1.notes FROM titles AS T1 INNER JOIN sales AS T2 ON T1.title_id = T2.title_id ORDER BY T2.qty DESC LIMIT 3

question: Provide calculate the total payment of products in order ID 10359.

WHERE od.`OrderID` = 10359;

question: What is the most common product ordered by a customer from Germany?

FROM `Customers`  c
JOIN `Orders`     o  ON c.`CustomerID` = o.`CustomerID`
JOIN `OrderDetails` od ON o.`OrderID` = od.`OrderID`
JOIN `Products`    p  ON od.`ProductID` = p.`ProductID`
WHERE c.`Country` = 'Germany'
GROUP BY p.`ProductID`, p.`ProductName`
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;
original_SQL: SELECT T2.ProductID FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN `Order Details` AS T2 WHERE T1.Country = 'Germany' GROUP BY T2.ProductID ORDER BY COUNT(T2.ProductID) DESC LIMIT 1

question: viral sinusitis condition refers to conditions.DESCRIPTION = 'Viral sinusitis (disorder)';

question: In the Catalan language, which biwords pair appeared the most in this language/page?
evidence: biwords pair refers to w1st.word w2nd.word; appeared the most refers to max(occurrences)
SQL: SELECT w1.`word` AS first_word, w2.`word` AS second_word FROM   `biwords` b JOIN   `words`   w1 ON w1.`wid` = b.`w1st` JOIN   `words`   w2 ON w2.`wid` = b.`w2nd` WHERE  b.`lid` = 1 AND  b.`occurrences` = ( SELECT MAX(`occurrences`) FROM   `biwords` WHERE  `lid` = 1)

question: What is the relationship between object sample no. 25 and object sample no. 2 on image no. 1?
evidence: relationship refers to PRED_CLASS; object sample no. 25 and object sample no. 2 refers to (OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID = 25 and OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID = 2) or (OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID = 2 and OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID = 25; image no. 1 refers to IMG_ID = 1
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT P.`PRED_CLASS`
FROM `IMG_REL` AS R
JOIN `PRED_CLASSES` AS P ON P.`PRED_CLASS_ID` = R.`PRED_CLASS_ID`
WHERE R.`IMG_ID` = 1
  AND ( (R.`OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID` = 25 AND R.`OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID` = 2)
        OR (R.`OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID` = 2  AND R.`OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID` = 25) );
original_evidence: relationship refers to PRED_CLASS; object sample no. 25 and object sample no. 2 refers to OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID = 25 and OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID = 2; image no. 1 refers to IMG_ID = 1
original_SQL: SELECT T2.PRED_CLASS FROM IMG_REL AS T1 INNER JOIN PRED_CLASSES AS T2 ON T1.PRED_CLASS_ID = T2.PRED_CLASS_ID WHERE T1.IMG_ID = 1 AND T1.OBJ1_SAMPLE_ID = 25 AND T1.OBJ2_SAMPLE_ID = 2

question: Among the Ivy League Schools in 2013, which schools have the highest number of Black students who graduated within 150 percent of normal/expected time who were seeking a bachelor's/equivalent cohort at 4-year institutions?
evidence: Ivy League Schools refers to chronname = 'Brown University' or chronname = 'Columbia University' or chronname = 'Cornell University' or chronname = 'Dartmouth College' or chronname = 'Harvard University' or chronname = 'Princeton University' or chronname = 'University of Pennsylvania' or chronname = 'Yale University'; in 2013 refers to year = '2013'; highest number of Black students who graduated within 150 percent of normal/expected time refers to MAX(grad_150 WHERE race = 'B'); seeking a bachelor's/equivalent cohort at 4-year institutions refers to cohort = '4y bach';
SQL:
WITH ivy AS (
    SELECT d.`chronname`,
           CAST(g.`grad_150` AS INTEGER) AS g150
    FROM `institution_details` d
    JOIN `institution_grads`  g ON g.`unitid` = d.`unitid`
    WHERE d.`chronname` IN ('Brown University','Columbia University','Cornell University',
                            'Dartmouth College','Harvard University','Princeton University',
                            'University of Pennsylvania','Yale University')
      AND g.`year`   = 2013
      AND g.`race`   = 'B'
      AND g.`cohort` = '4y bach'
      AND g.`gender` = 'B'  
)
SELECT `chronname`
FROM ivy
WHERE g150 = (SELECT MAX(g150) FROM ivy);
original_SQL: SELECT T1.chronname FROM institution_details AS T1 INNER JOIN institution_grads AS T2 ON T2.unitid = T1.unitid WHERE T1.chronname IN ( 'Brown University', 'Columbia University', 'Cornell University', 'Dartmouth College', 'Harvard University', 'Princeton University', 'University of Pennsylvania', 'Yale University' ) AND T2.year = 2013 AND T2.race = 'B' AND T2.cohort = '4y bach' ORDER BY T2.grad_cohort DESC LIMIT 1

question: Provide the registered courses' names by undergraduate students with GPA of 3.7 and above.
evidence: Undergraduate students refers to type = 'UG'; GPA of 3.7 and above refers to gpa >= 3.7;
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT `c`.`name`
FROM `student` AS `s`
JOIN `registration` AS `r` ON `s`.`student_id` = `r`.`student_id`
JOIN `course` AS `c` ON `r`.`course_id` = `c`.`course_id`
WHERE `s`.`type` = 'UG' AND `s`.`gpa` >= 3.7;
original_evidence: Undergraduate students refers to type = 'UG'; GPA of 3.7 and above refers to gpa > 3.7;
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.f_name FROM student AS T1 INNER JOIN registration AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id INNER JOIN course AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T1.type = 'UG' AND T1.gpa > 3.7

question: Which NBA team that didn't play in playoffs had the most winning rate in the 2000 NBA regular season? List the team id.
evidence: NBA refers to lgID = 'NBA'; didn't play in the playoffs refers to PostGP = 0; 2000 refers to year = 2000; the most winning rate refers to max(divide(won, add(won, lost)))
SQL:
WITH team_win_rate AS (
  SELECT T2.tmID, CAST(T2.won AS REAL) / (T2.won + T2.lost) win_rate 
  FROM players_teams AS T1 
  INNER JOIN teams AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year 
  WHERE T1.PostGP = 0 AND T1.year = 2000
) SELECT DISTINCT tmID 
FROM team_win_rate
WHERE win_rate = (SELECT MAX(win_rate) FROM team_win_rate)
original_question: Which NBA team that didn't play in playoffs had the most winning rate in the 2000 NBA regular season?
original_SQL: SELECT T2.tmID FROM players_teams AS T1 INNER JOIN teams AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year WHERE T1.PostGP = 0 AND T1.year = 2000 ORDER BY CAST(T2.won AS REAL) / (T2.won + T2.lost) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Please list the three episodes with the highest number of votes for the worst star rating.

question: What is the percentage of the number of 4-year public schools in Madison Area Technical College's home state?
evidence: 4-year refers to level = '4-year'; public refers to control = 'Public'; Madison Area Technical College refers to chronname = 'Madison Area Technical College'; home state in the United States refers to state;
SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN level = '4-year' AND control = 'Public' THEN 1 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) FROM institution_details WHERE state = ( SELECT state FROM institution_details WHERE chronname = 'Madison Area Technical College' )
original_question: What is the percentage of the number of 4-year public schools from Madison Area Technical College's home state in the Alabama?
original_evidence: percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(chronname = 'Madison Area Technical College'), SUM(state = 'Alabama')), 100); 4-year refers to level = '4-year'; public refers to control = 'Public'; Madison Area Technical College refers to chronname = 'Madison Area Technical College'; home state in the United States refers to state;
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T1.state = ( SELECT T1.state FROM institution_details AS T1 INNER JOIN state_sector_details AS T2 ON T2.state = T1.state WHERE T1.chronname = 'Madison Area Technical College' ) AND T1.level = '4-year' AND T1.control = 'Public' THEN T1.chronname ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T2.state = 'Alabama' THEN T1.chronname ELSE NULL END) FROM institution_details AS T1 INNER JOIN state_sector_details AS T2 ON T2.state = T1.state

question: What is the percentage ratio of students who are enlisted in foreign legions that have a due payment?
evidence: have a due payment refers to bool = 'pos'; in foreign legions refers to organ = 'foreign_legion'; ratio refers to DIVIDE(COUNT(bool = 'pos'), COUNT(bool = 'neg' OR bool = 'pos'))*100
SQL:
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN n.`bool` = 'pos' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS percent_with_due
FROM   `no_payment_due` AS n
WHERE  n.`name` IN (SELECT DISTINCT `name`
                    FROM `enlist`
                    WHERE `organ` = 'foreign_legion');
original_evidence: have a due payment refers to bool = 'pos'; in foreign legions refers to organ = 'foreign_legion'; ratio refers to DIVIDE(COUNT(bool = 'pos'), COUNT(bool = 'neg'))
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.bool = 'pos', 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T1.bool = 'neg', 1, 0)) FROM no_payment_due AS T1 INNER JOIN enlist AS T2 ON T1.`name` = T2.`name` WHERE T2.organ = 'foreign_legion'

question: How many American restaurants are located in Front, San Francisco?
evidence: American restaurant refers to food_type = 'american'; Front refers to street_name = 'front'; San Francisco refers to city = 'san francisco'
SQL:
SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM `location` AS l
JOIN `generalinfo` AS g ON l.`id_restaurant` = g.`id_restaurant`
WHERE l.`city` = 'san francisco'
  AND l.`street_name` = 'front'
  AND g.`food_type` = 'american';
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.food_type = 'american') FROM location AS T1 INNER JOIN generalinfo AS T2 ON T1.id_restaurant = T2.id_restaurant WHERE T1.city = 'san francisco' AND T1.street_name = 'front'

question: How many docks were available at the starting station of trip ID 4312 when the trip started?
SQL:
SELECT SUM(T2.docks_available) 
FROM trip AS T1 
INNER JOIN status AS T2 
ON T2.station_id = T1.start_station_id
AND (T2.time LIKE SUBSTR(T1.start_date, 6, 4) || '/%' || SUBSTR(T1.start_date, 1, 1) || '/' || SUBSTR(T1.start_date, 3, 2) || ' ' || SUBSTR(T1.start_date, 11) || '%')   
WHERE T1.id = 4312
original_question: How many docks were available at the starting station of trip ID 912900?
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(T2.docks_available) FROM trip AS T1 INNER JOIN status AS T2 ON T2.station_id = T1.start_station_id WHERE T1.zip_code = 912900

question: Among the employees who wish to receive e-mail promotion from AdventureWorks, how many percent of them are female?
evidence: female refers to Gender = 'F'; employee who wish to receive email promotion refers to EmailPromotion = 1; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(Gender = 'F')), (sum(Gender = 'F' or Gender = 'M'))) as percentage;
SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN e.Gender = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS FemalePercent FROM Employee e JOIN Person p ON e.BusinessEntityID = p.BusinessEntityID WHERE p.EmailPromotion IN (1, 2)
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.Gender = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.BusinessEntityID) FROM Employee AS T1 INNER JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T2.EmailPromotion = 1

question: Which of the two courses, "Advanced Operating System" or "Intro to BlockChain', did most of the students receive an A in?

question: Among the crimes, what percentage are severe?
evidence: severe refers to index_code = 'I'; percentage = divide(count(iucr_no where index_code = 'I'), count(iucr_no)) * 100%
SQL: SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN i.`index_code` = 'I' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS severe_percentage FROM `Crime` c JOIN `IUCR` i ON c.`iucr_no` = i.`iucr_no`
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN index_code = 'I' THEN iucr_no ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(iucr_no) FROM IUCR

question: Which Photography app has the highest total Sentiment subjectivity score?
evidence: Photography app refers to Genre = 'Photography'; highest total sentiment subjectivity score = MAX(sum(Sentiment_Subjectivity));
SQL:
WITH photography_apps AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT `App`
    FROM playstore
    WHERE `Genres` = 'Photography'
)
SELECT ur.`App`
FROM user_reviews ur
JOIN photography_apps pa ON pa.`App` = ur.`App`
GROUP BY ur.`App`
ORDER BY SUM(CAST(ur.`Sentiment_Subjectivity` AS REAL)) DESC
LIMIT 1;
original_SQL: SELECT T1.App FROM playstore AS T1 INNER JOIN user_reviews AS T2 ON T1.App = T2.App WHERE T1.Genres = 'Photography' GROUP BY T1.App ORDER BY SUM(T2.Sentiment_Subjectivity) DESC LIMIT 1

question: List the films' titles which were rented by Brian Wyman in July, 2005.

question: What is the percentage of player who won "All-Defensive First Team" from 1980 - 2000 is from 'NY'.
evidence: "All-Defensive First Team" is the award; ' NY' is the birthState; 1980 to 2000 refers to year between 1980 and 2000; percentage = Divide (Count(playerID where birthState = 'NY'), Count(playerID)) * 100
SQL:
WITH winners AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT `playerID`
  FROM `awards_players`
  WHERE `award` = 'All-Defensive First Team' AND `year` BETWEEN 1980 AND 2000
),
ny_winners AS (
  SELECT w.`playerID`
  FROM winners w
  JOIN `players` p ON p.`playerID` = w.`playerID`
  WHERE p.`birthState` = 'NY'
)
SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM winners) AS pct_ny_winners;
original_SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.playerID) FROM players AS T1 INNER JOIN awards_players AS T2 ON T1.playerID = T2.playerID WHERE T1.birthState = 'NY' AND T2.award = 'All-Defensive First Team' AND T2.year BETWEEN 1980 AND 2000

question: What was the inspection type when El Aji Peruvian Restaurant got highest inspection score?
evidence: El Aji Peruvian Restaurant is the name of the business; highest inspection score refers to MAX(score);
SQL:
WITH max_score AS (
    SELECT MAX(i.score) AS top_score
    FROM inspections i
    JOIN businesses b ON b.business_id = i.business_id
    WHERE b.name = 'El Aji Peruvian Restaurant'
)
SELECT DISTINCT i.type
FROM inspections i
JOIN businesses b ON b.business_id = i.business_id
JOIN max_score m ON i.score = m.top_score
WHERE b.name = 'El Aji Peruvian Restaurant';
original_SQL: SELECT T1.type FROM inspections AS T1 INNER JOIN businesses AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T2.name = 'El Aji Peruvian Restaurant' ORDER BY T1.score DESC LIMIT 1

question: Among the lists created by user 4208563, which ones have the highest number of followers? Indicate the list ids, how many followers they have, and whether the users were a subscriber or not when they created the lists.
evidence: User 4208563 refers to user_id;highest number of followers refers to MAX(list_followers); user_subscriber = 1 means that the user was a subscriber when he created the list; user_subscriber = 0 means the user was not a subscriber when he created the list (to replace); return 1 if the user was a subscriber when the list was created. Otherwise, return 0.
SQL: SELECT T2.list_id, T1.list_followers, T2.user_subscriber = 1 FROM lists AS T1 INNER JOIN lists_users AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id AND T2.list_id = T2.list_id WHERE T2.user_id = 4208563 AND T1.list_followers = (SELECT MAX(T1.list_followers) FROM lists AS T1 INNER JOIN lists_users AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id AND T2.list_id = T2.list_id WHERE T2.user_id = 4208563);
original_question: Among the lists created by user 4208563, which one has the highest number of followers? Indicate how many followers it has and whether the user was a subscriber or not when he created the list.
original_evidence: User 4208563 refers to user_id;highest number of followers refers to MAX(list_followers); user_subscriber = 1 means that the user was a subscriber when he created the list; user_subscriber = 0 means the user was not a subscriber when he created the list (to replace)
original_SQL: SELECT T1.list_followers, T2.user_subscriber = 1 FROM lists AS T1 INNER JOIN lists_users AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id AND T2.list_id = T2.list_id WHERE T2.user_id = 4208563 ORDER BY T1.list_followers DESC LIMIT 1

question: Among the trains running west, how many trains have no more than one car with an open roof?
evidence: running west refers to direction = 'west'; open roof refers to roof = 'none'
SQL:
WITH one_car AS (
  SELECT t.id AS id
  FROM trains  AS t
  JOIN cars AS c ON t.id = c.train_id
  WHERE t.direction = 'west' AND c.roof = 'none'
  GROUP BY t.id
  HAVING COUNT(c.id) <= 1),
zero_car AS (
  SELECT t.id AS id
  FROM trains  AS t
  LEFT JOIN cars AS c ON t.id = c.train_id AND c.roof = 'none'
  WHERE t.direction = 'west' AND c.train_id IS NULL
  GROUP BY t.id)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT id) FROM (SELECT id FROM one_car UNION SELECT id FROM zero_car);
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN T1.direction = 'west' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)as count FROM trains AS T1 INNER JOIN ( SELECT train_id, COUNT(id) FROM cars WHERE roof = 'none' GROUP BY train_id HAVING COUNT(id) = 1 ) AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.train_id

question: Among customers with the last name of Alvarez, who purchased the highest quantity? Report the customer id.
evidence: highest quantity refers to MAX(Quantity);
SQL: SELECT c.CustomerID FROM Customers AS c JOIN Sales AS s ON c.CustomerID = s.CustomerID WHERE c.LastName = 'Alvarez' GROUP BY c.CustomerID HAVING SUM(s.Quantity) = (SELECT MAX(total_quantity) FROM (SELECT c2.CustomerID, SUM(s2.Quantity) AS total_quantity FROM Customers AS c2 JOIN Sales AS s2 ON c2.CustomerID = s2.CustomerID WHERE c2.LastName = 'Alvarez' GROUP BY c2.CustomerID) AS subquery);
original_question: Among customers with the last name of Valdez, who purchased the highest quantity?
original_SQL: SELECT T1.FirstName FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN Sales AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.LastName = 'Valdez' ORDER BY T2.Quantity DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which year had the most number of trips that started at stations in San Francisco?
evidence: started at station refers to start_station_name; San Francisco refers to city = 'San Francisco'; year that had the most number of trips refers to MAX(year(start_date));
SQL:
SELECT year
FROM (
    SELECT SUBSTR(`start_date`, INSTR(`start_date`, ' ')-4, 4) AS year,
           COUNT(*) AS trip_count
    FROM `trip`
    JOIN `station` ON `station`.`id` = `trip`.`start_station_id`
    WHERE `station`.`city` = 'San Francisco'
    GROUP BY year
    ORDER BY trip_count DESC
    LIMIT 1
);
original_SQL: SELECT SUBSTR(CAST(T1.start_date AS TEXT), INSTR(T1.start_date, ' '), -4) FROM trip AS T1 INNER JOIN station AS T2 ON T2.name = T1.start_station_name WHERE T2.city = 'San Francisco' GROUP BY T1.start_station_name ORDER BY COUNT(T1.id) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which customer addresses are no longer active?
evidence: no longer active refers to address_status = 'Inactive'; customer address refers to street_number, street_name, city
SQL:
SELECT a.`street_number`,
       a.`street_name`,
       a.`city`
FROM   `address`          AS a
JOIN   `customer_address` AS ca ON ca.`address_id` = a.`address_id`
JOIN   `address_status`   AS s  ON s.`status_id`   = ca.`status_id`
WHERE  LOWER(s.`address_status`) = 'inactive';
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.street_name FROM address AS T1 INNER JOIN customer_address AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id INNER JOIN address_status AS T3 ON T3.status_id = T2.status_id WHERE T3.address_status = 'Inactive'

question: Are there any post-production movies in Nederlands? 'YES' or 'NO'
evidence: post-production movies refers to movie_status = 'Post Production'; Nederlands refers to language_name = 'Nederlands';
SQL:
SELECT CASE
         WHEN EXISTS ( SELECT 1
                       FROM `movie` m
                       JOIN `movie_languages` ml ON m.`movie_id` = ml.`movie_id`
                       JOIN `language` l ON ml.`language_id` = l.`language_id`
                       WHERE m.`movie_status` = 'Post Production'
                         AND l.`language_name` = 'Nederlands')
         THEN 'YES'
         ELSE 'NO'
       END AS answer;
original_question: Are there any post-production movies in Nederlands?
original_SQL: SELECT DISTINCT CASE WHEN T1.movie_status = 'Post Production' THEN 'YES' ELSE 'NO' END AS YORN FROM movie AS T1 INNER JOIN movie_languages AS T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id INNER JOIN language AS T3 ON T2.language_id = T3.language_id WHERE T3.language_name = 'Nederlands'

question: Provide the ID of professors who are teaching high-level or harder undergraduate course.
evidence: ID of professors refers to taughtBy.p_id; high-level or harder undergraduate course refers to courseLevel = 'Level_400'
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT tb.`p_id`
FROM `taughtBy` tb
JOIN `course`  c ON c.`course_id` = tb.`course_id`
JOIN `person`  p ON p.`p_id` = tb.`p_id`
WHERE c.`courseLevel` = 'Level_400'
  AND p.`professor` = 1;
original_SQL: SELECT T2.p_id FROM course AS T1 INNER JOIN taughtBy AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T1.courseLevel = 'Level_400'

question: Among the restaurants being inspected in 2016, how many of them are in San Francisco?
evidence: inspected in 2016 refers to YEAR(date) = 2016; San Francisco refers to city in ('San Francisco', 'SF' ,'S.F.', 'SAN FRANCISCO');
SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.business_id) FROM inspections AS T1 INNER JOIN businesses AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T1.`date`) = '2016' AND T2.city IN ('San Francisco', 'SAN FRANCISCO', 'SF', 'S.F.')

question: What is the average number of crimes in a neighborhood in Central Chicago?
evidence: Central Chicago refers to side = 'Central'; average number = divide(count(report_no), count(neighborhood_name))
SQL:
SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) / (SELECT COUNT(*)
                                  FROM `Neighborhood`
                                  WHERE `community_area_no` IN (SELECT `community_area_no`
                                                               FROM `Community_Area`
                                                               WHERE `side` = 'Central')) AS avg_crimes_per_neighborhood
FROM `Crime` AS c
JOIN `Community_Area` AS ca ON c.`community_area_no` = ca.`community_area_no`
WHERE ca.`side` = 'Central';
original_evidence: Central Chicago refers to side = 'Central'; average number = divide(count(report_no), count(community_area_no))
original_SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(T1.report_no) AS REAL) / COUNT(T2.community_area_no) FROM Crime AS T1 INNER JOIN Community_Area AS T2 ON T1.community_area_no = T2.community_area_no WHERE T2.side = 'Central'

question: Which solution contains files within a more popular repository, the solution ID18 or solution ID19?
evidence: more watchers mean that this repository is more popular;
SQL: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Id = 18 THEN T1.Watchers ELSE 0 END) > SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Id = 19 THEN T1.Watchers ELSE 0 END) THEN 'solution ID18' WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Id = 18 THEN T1.Watchers ELSE 0 END) < SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Id = 19 THEN T1.Watchers ELSE 0 END) THEN 'solution ID19' END isMorePopular FROM Repo AS T1 INNER JOIN Solution AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.RepoId
original_SQL: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Id = 18 THEN T1.Watchers ELSE 0 END) > SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Id = 19 THEN T1.Watchers ELSE 0 END) THEN 'SolutionID18' WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Id = 18 THEN T1.Watchers ELSE 0 END) < SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Id = 19 THEN T1.Watchers ELSE 0 END) THEN 'SolutionID19' END isMorePopular FROM Repo AS T1 INNER JOIN Solution AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.RepoId

question: Which character has the longest screen time in the movie Batman?
evidence: longest screen time refers to max(screentime); movie Batman refers to title = 'Batman'
SQL:
SELECT T2.`Character Name` FROM movie AS T1 INNER JOIN characters AS T2 ON T1.MovieID = T2.MovieID WHERE T1.Title = 'Batman' AND T2.screentime = 
(SELECT MAX(T4.screentime) FROM movie AS T3 INNER JOIN characters AS T4 ON T3.MovieID = T4.MovieID WHERE T3.Title = 'Batman')
original_SQL: SELECT T2.`Character Name` FROM movie AS T1 INNER JOIN characters AS T2 ON T1.MovieID = T2.MovieID WHERE T1.Title = 'Batman' ORDER BY T2.screentime DESC LIMIT 1

question: How many days delay for the complaint call from Mr. Brantley Julian Stanley on 2012/5/18?
evidence: days delay for the complaint = SUBTRACT("date sent to company", "Date received"); Mr refers to sex = 'Male'; on 2012/5/18 refers to "Date received" = '2012-05-18';
SQL:
SELECT CAST( julianday(e.`Date sent to company`) -
             julianday(e.`Date received`) AS INT) AS days_delay
FROM   events  AS e
JOIN   client  AS c ON c.client_id = e.`Client_ID`
WHERE  e.`Date received` = '2012-05-18'
  AND  c.first  = 'Brantley'
  AND  c.middle = 'Julian'
  AND  c.last   = 'Stanley'
  AND  c.sex    = 'Male'
original_SQL: SELECT 365 * (strftime('%Y', T2.`Date sent to company`) - strftime('%Y', T2.`Date received`)) + 30 * (strftime('%M', T2.`Date sent to company`) - strftime('%M', T2.`Date received`)) + (strftime('%d', T2.`Date sent to company`) - strftime('%d', T2.`Date received`)) AS days FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN events AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.Client_ID WHERE T2.`Date received` = '2012-05-18' AND T1.sex = 'Male' AND T1.first = 'Brantley' AND T1.middle = 'Julian' AND T1.last = 'Stanley'

question: What was the total amount of sales handled by Nancy Davolio in December 1996?
evidence: in December 1996 refers to year(OrderDate) = 1996 AND month(OrderDate) = 12; total amount of sales refers to MULTIPLY((UnitPrice, Quantity))
SQL:
SELECT SUM(p.`Price` * od.`Quantity`) AS total_sales
FROM `Employees`  e
JOIN `Orders`     o  ON e.`EmployeeID` = o.`EmployeeID`
JOIN `OrderDetails` od ON o.`OrderID` = od.`OrderID`
JOIN `Products`   p  ON od.`ProductID` = p.`ProductID`
WHERE e.`FirstName` = 'Nancy'
  AND e.`LastName`  = 'Davolio'
  AND strftime('%Y', o.`OrderDate`) = '1996'
  AND strftime('%m', o.`OrderDate`) = '12';
original_question: What was the total amount of sales handled by Nancy Davolio in December 1996, excluding discounts?
original_evidence: in December 1996 refers to year(OrderDate) = 1996 AND month(OrderDate) = 12; excluding discounts refers to Discount = 0; total amount of sales refers to MULTIPLY((UnitPrice, Quantity))
original_SQL: SELECT SUM(T3.UnitPrice * T3.Quantity) FROM Employees AS T1 INNER JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.EmployeeID INNER JOIN `Order Details` AS T3 ON T2.OrderID = T3.OrderID WHERE T1.FirstName = 'Nancy' AND T1.LastName = 'Davolio' AND T2.OrderDate LIKE '1996-12%' AND T3.Discount = 0

question: What is the profit for part no.98768 in order no.1?
evidence: part no.98768 refers to l_partkey = 98768; order no.1 refers to l_orderkey = 1; profit = subtract(multiply(l_extendedprice, subtract(1, l_discount)), multiply(ps_supplycost, l_quantity))
SQL:
SELECT (l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount) ) - (ps_supplycost * l_quantity) AS profit
FROM `lineitem`  
JOIN `partsupp` ON `lineitem`.`l_partkey` = `partsupp`.`ps_partkey`
               AND `lineitem`.`l_suppkey` = `partsupp`.`ps_suppkey`
WHERE `l_orderkey` = 1 AND `l_partkey` = 98768;
original_SQL: SELECT T1.l_extendedprice * (1 - T1.l_discount) - T2.ps_supplycost * T1.l_quantity FROM lineitem AS T1 INNER JOIN partsupp AS T2 ON T1.l_suppkey = T2.ps_suppkey WHERE T1.l_orderkey = 1 AND T1.l_partkey = 98768

question: How frequently did the words 1 and 25 appear together?
evidence: How frequently refers to occurrences;  word 1 refers to wid = 1; word 25 refers to wid = 25; appear together means pair, which is a relationship of two words: w1st and w2nd, where w1st is word id of the first word and w2nd is a word id of the second word;  w1st or w2nd = wid = 1; w1st or w2nd = wid = 25;
SQL: SELECT SUM(`occurrences`) AS frequency FROM `biwords` WHERE (`w1st` = 1 AND `w2nd` = 25) OR (`w1st` = 25 AND `w2nd` = 1)
original_SQL: SELECT occurrences FROM biwords WHERE w1st = 1 AND w2nd = 25

question: What product is the least shipped to the postal code 28023?
evidence: the least shipped product refers ProductName where MIN(SUM(Quantity));
SQL:
SELECT p.`ProductName`
FROM `Customers`  c
JOIN `Orders`    o  ON c.`CustomerID` = o.`CustomerID`
JOIN `OrderDetails` od ON o.`OrderID`   = od.`OrderID`
JOIN `Products`   p  ON od.`ProductID` = p.`ProductID`
WHERE c.`PostalCode` = '28023'
GROUP BY p.`ProductID`
ORDER BY SUM(od.`Quantity`) ASC
LIMIT 1;
original_evidence: the least shipped product refers ProductName where MIN(Quantity);
original_SQL: SELECT T4.ProductName FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID INNER JOIN `Order Details` AS T3 ON T2.OrderID = T3.OrderID INNER JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.ProductID = T4.ProductID WHERE T1.PostalCode = 28023 ORDER BY T3.Quantity LIMIT 1
